question_text: What causes gas pressure according to the particle model of matter?

options.label: A
options.text: Gas particles colliding with the walls of a container

options.label: B
options.text: The weight of gas pushing down from above

options.label: C
options.text: The colour of the gas molecules

options.label: D
options.text: The temperature of the container walls

explanation: Gas pressure is caused by gas particles moving randomly and colliding with the walls of their container. Each tiny collision contributes to the overall pressure we measure.
